symptom: Direct Edit/Write on OneDrive .py files causes EEXIST errors and corruption ( -> ). Hook enforces safe pattern.
cause: OneDrive file sync + CRLF line ending handling creates race conditions when writing .py files directly
solution: Always use safe patching pattern: (1) Write to C:/Users/brian/AppData/Local/Temp/script.py (2) Run python to execute and apply patches (3) Script uses read_bytes()->normalize CRLF->patch->write_bytes(). See .claude/rules/tooling-issues-workflow.md

symptom: Files named with hyphens (e.g. 'nielsen-connector.py') cannot be imported as Python modules: ModuleNotFoundError
cause: Python module names cannot contain hyphens; Python sees hyphen as subtraction operator
solution: Rename files to use underscores instead: 'nielsen-connector.py' -> 'nielsen_connector.py'. Both human-readable on disk AND importable in code.

symptom_short: Hook looks in wrong directory context
symptom: Edit/Write tools trigger hook that looks for .claude/hooks/ relative to current working directory instead of repo root
cause: Hook executed from script's directory context, not from repo root
solution: Always use Bash with proper cd or absolute paths. When using Edit/Write on OneDrive files, use safe patching pattern (temp file -> bash copy) instead

symptom: ClientSecretCredential.get_token failed with AADSTS7000222: provided client secret keys are expired. Nielsen SQL connection fails.
cause: Azure AD client secrets have limited lifetime (typically 6-24 months). Credentials need periodic rotation.
solution: Regenerate RU_CLIENT_SECRET in Azure Portal: App registrations → find app ID f0460586-525d-4d1e-adb7-c5b6dfd26aab → Certificates & secrets → create new client secret → copy value → update RU_CLIENT_SECRET in .env. Self-service; no need to contact Manifold.

symptom: UnicodeEncodeError when printing filenames with non-ASCII characters (e.g., González, cedilla) to terminal
cause: Windows cmd.exe defaults to cp1252 encoding which doesn't support accented characters in filenames; Python uses UTF-8 internally but terminal expects cp1252
solution: Use PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 environment variable when running scripts, or handle encoding errors gracefully with errors='replace' in print statements

symptom_short: pytest async fixtures not supported in async tests
symptom: pytest raises PytestRemovedIn9Warning when async test function uses async fixture; results in ERROR at fixture setup
cause: pytest doesn't natively support async fixtures as of 9.0.3; need pytest-asyncio plugin and proper fixture decoration
solution: Use @pytest_asyncio.fixture decorator for async fixtures, or use synchronous fixtures with sync setup code
